programs.name: Research Science Institute (RSI)
programs.host: MIT / Center for Excellence in Education
programs.desc: The most prestigious STEM summer program in the US. 100 rising seniors conduct original research mentored by MIT scientists in labs across Boston. ~3,000 applicants, ~100 admitted (~3%). Covers the full research cycle: coursework, lab work, and a formal conference-style presentation. Fully free — tuition, housing, meals all covered.
programs.note: Applications for 2026 are closed (deadline was Dec 10, 2025). Applications for RSI 2027 typically open in October 2026 at cee.org.

programs.name: MITES Summer
programs.desc: Rigorous 6-week residential program for rising seniors passionate about STEM. Acceptance rate 1.5–4% — among the most selective HS programs in the US. Five college-level courses in math, life sciences, physics, engineering, and a project elective. Prioritizes underrepresented students but all US citizens/permanent residents may apply. All costs covered.
programs.note: Applications for 2026 are closed (deadline Feb 2). Apply in fall of junior year for 2027 — same application as MITES Semester.

programs.name: PROMYS
programs.desc: One of the most prestigious math programs in the world. ~75 students spend 6 weeks exploring deep number theory and proof-based mathematics as practicing mathematicians. Application requires solving a rigorous problem set. Must have completed 9th grade and be at least 14. Free for families earning under $80k.
programs.note: 2026 application is closed. Applications for PROMYS 2027 open in January 2027 at promys.org.

programs.name: HCSSiM
programs.desc: A legendary 6-week math summer program in its 54th year. ~51 students explore college-level mathematics with unparalleled faculty access. 4-hour morning classes Mon–Sat plus evening problem sessions. Application includes the 'Very Interesting Test' (already sent to all who apply). Rolling decisions — closes when full. Free for families under $85k.
programs.note: Still accepting! Rolling decisions — apply immediately at hcssim.org. The 2026 Interesting Test has been sent to all applicants. Deadline for full consideration is late April.

programs.name: MathROOTS
programs.desc: A free 2-week residential math accelerator at MIT for high-potential students from underrepresented backgrounds. Rigorous combinatorics, number theory, and geometry at a college level. Nationally selective; housing and meals fully covered. Strongly encourages Black, Hispanic, and Indigenous applicants.
programs.note: 2026 deadline was March 3. Open to students completing 9th or 10th grade. Apply for 2027 in early spring.

programs.name: TASS (Telluride Summer Seminar)
programs.desc: One of the most selective humanities programs in the US. ~30 students per program attend 5-week college-level seminars in Critical Black Studies (CBS) or Anti-Oppressive Studies (AOS). Tuition, books, room, board, field trips, and travel all covered. Open to sophomores and juniors (ages 15–17). Acceptance rate ~3–5%.
programs.note: 2026 deadline was December 3, 2025. Applications for TASS 2027 open in October 2026.

programs.name: Simons Summer Research Program
programs.desc: Rising seniors conduct hands-on research with Stony Brook faculty in science, math, or engineering. Must be nominated by school (max 2 per school). Acceptance rate below 5%. Concludes with a research poster and stipend award. No tuition charged. US citizens and permanent residents only.
programs.note: Applications closed. Requires school nomination. Stipend provided at conclusion. Apply in January of junior year for 2027.

programs.name: Summer Science Program (SSP)
programs.desc: One of the oldest and most prestigious STEM programs in the US (since 1959). Rising seniors spend 5 weeks doing authentic research in Astrophysics, Biochemistry, Bacterial Genomics, or Cell Biology. Teams of 3, ~700 total spots. Need-blind admissions with generous financial aid — many attend free. Alumni network of 4,300+ scientists and engineers.
programs.note: 2026 domestic deadline was Feb 19. Need-blind admissions means cost is based on family income. Apply in junior year for 2027. SSP is comparable in prestige to RSI and MITES.

programs.name: Broad Summer Scholars Program (BSSP)
programs.desc: Rising seniors from the Greater Boston area conduct cutting-edge research at the Broad Institute (affiliated with MIT and Harvard). Research areas: cancer biology, psychiatric disease, computational genomics, infectious disease, and more. Free plus a $3,600 stipend. No prior research experience required. ~20–30 students selected.
programs.note: 2026 deadline was January 21. Limited to students attending high school in the Greater Boston area. Free with a $3,600 stipend.

programs.name: NASA SEES Internship
programs.host: NASA / UT Austin Center for Space Research
programs.desc: Sophomores and juniors (must be 16 by July 5) analyze real NASA satellite data alongside NASA scientists. Includes a 2-week residential stay at UT Austin with housing and meals. Virtual science symposium July 20–21. ~215 students selected from ~2,000 applicants (~10%). US citizens only.
programs.note: 2026 deadline closed Feb 22. Must be in 10th or 11th grade. $2,000 cost — scholarship available for financial need.

programs.name: MIT Beaver Works Summer Institute
programs.desc: Rising seniors complete free online prerequisite courses (open since Dec 2025), then apply for the 4-week summer session. 14+ tracks including autonomous vehicles, quantum computing, cybersecurity, satellite design, ML, and hardware hacking. Free for families earning under $90k; up to $2,350 for higher incomes. No housing provided.
programs.note: Still open — must have registered for and completed the online course first. Register immediately at bwsi.mit.edu if you haven't already.

programs.name: Carnegie Mellon SAMS
programs.desc: 6-week residential program at CMU for rising seniors from underrepresented backgrounds. Full scholarship: tuition, housing, meals, and a stipend. Prioritizes first-generation college students and students from underserved communities.
programs.note: Deadline passed. Open to US citizens/permanent residents from underrepresented backgrounds. Fully funded.

programs.name: COSMOS — CA Summer School for Math & Science
programs.loc: UC Davis, UC San Diego, UC Irvine, UC Santa Cruz
programs.desc: 4-week residential program for students passionate about STEM. 12+ cluster tracks per campus including AI/ML, astrophysics, bioengineering, marine science, robotics, and math. Led by UC faculty. Priority to California residents, but nationally open.
programs.note: 2026 applications closed Feb 6. Apply in January of 9th–11th grade for 2027.

programs.name: UIUC Young Scholars Program
programs.desc: 6-week research experience for rising 10th–12th graders with UIUC faculty mentors. Research areas include AI, cancer immunology, quantum mechanics, materials science, and neuroscience. Housing, meals, and a monetary award provided. Priority to students from IL, IN, KY, MI, MO, IA, WI.
programs.note: Deadline closed. Midwest priority but all US students are eligible. Apply in February of sophomore or junior year.

programs.name: MITES Semester (Online)
programs.desc: Free 6-month online STEM program for rising seniors, designed as an alternative to MITES Summer. Rigorous online courses, virtual workshops, and college admissions counseling. Same application as MITES Summer — you are offered one or the other, not both.
programs.note: Same application and deadline as MITES Summer (Feb 2). If admitted, you receive a single offer — Summer or Semester.

programs.name: Yale Young Global Scholars (YYGS)
programs.desc: Academically intensive 2-week program in 8 interdisciplinary tracks including Applied Science, Biological Science, Frontiers of Medicine, International Affairs & Security, Law & Politics, and Sustainability. 1,600+ students from 120+ countries per year. Strong need-based aid.
programs.note: 2026 deadline was January 7. Need-based aid covers full cost for qualifying families. Apply in fall/early winter for 2027.

programs.name: Economics for Leaders (EFL)
programs.desc: Competitive week-long program at universities nationwide. Future leaders study how economic analysis applies to real policy decisions. ~2 applicants accepted per school. Fully funded: tuition, room, and board. Nominations often come through economics or social studies teachers.
programs.note: Deadline passed. Ask your economics or social studies teacher about nominations for 2027.

programs.name: Bank of America Student Leaders
programs.desc: Paid 8-week internship at a local nonprofit at ~$18/hr plus a week-long leadership summit in Washington, DC. One of the few fully paid internship programs open to high schoolers. Develops community leadership skills and nonprofit sector experience.
programs.note: 2026 deadline was March 9. Check your local Bank of America branch website for city-specific 2027 deadlines.

programs.name: Duke Pre-College Summer Programs
programs.desc: 1–3 week intensive on-campus sessions taught by Duke faculty. All 2026 programs are in-person only (no online option). Topics include neuroscience, AI, marine biology, engineering design, and global health. Must be at least 16 by June 29, 2026. 3.0+ GPA required.
programs.note: Still accepting! Rolling admissions — sessions fill up quickly so apply soon. Must be 16+ by June 29. No online option in 2026.

programs.name: UCSB Early Research Scholars Program
programs.desc: Students earn 4 UC transferable college credits through rigorous research at UCSB. 12+ tracks including neuroscience, CS, marine biology, physics, and chemistry. 3.5+ GPA recommended. Students write a formal research paper and present at a closing symposium.
programs.note: Deadline passed. Check for rolling spots. Partial aid available for CA residents.

programs.name: Stanford Summer Humanities Institute
programs.desc: A 3-week residential program where students engage with big questions in humanities, social science, and law at Stanford. Daily seminars led by Stanford faculty. Selective; strong need-based financial aid program.
programs.note: Deadline passed. Strong financial aid available. Separate from Stanford Pre-Collegiate Studies.

programs.name: JHU Center for Talented Youth (CTY)
programs.desc: Wide range of intensive academic courses for academically talented students. Residential programs at JHU and partner colleges, plus online sessions. Courses span STEM, writing, humanities, and research. Designed for advanced learners. Multiple financial aid options available.
programs.note: Still accepting! Many 2026 sessions still available. Check cty.jhu.edu for specific course availability.

programs.name: Harvard Pre-College Program
programs.desc: Two-week residential program where rising juniors and seniors (must be 16–18) take Harvard courses without the pressure of grades. Three sessions offered. $6,100 covers tuition, room, meals, and insurance. Limited need-based scholarships. Must graduate in 2027 or 2028.
programs.note: Still accepting applications on a rolling basis. Must be at least 16 by June 20, 2026 and not yet 19 by July 31. Apply at summer.harvard.edu.

programs.name: Cornell Summer College
programs.desc: Take actual Cornell undergraduate courses for 3–8 weeks and earn transferable college credit. Programs in engineering, architecture, business, law, life sciences, and liberal arts. Full campus experience on Cornell's scenic Ithaca campus.
programs.note: Rolling admissions — open now. Limited financial aid. Credit is transferable to most US colleges and universities.

programs.name: Georgetown Pre-College Programs
programs.desc: Summer programs in government, international affairs, pre-law, business, and STEM on Georgetown's historic campus in Washington, DC. DC location adds unique access to policy briefings, government agencies, and international organizations.
programs.note: Deadline is April 15, 2026 — apply now! DC location makes this unique for students interested in policy, law, or government.

programs.name: Davidson Academy Online
programs.desc: Free online accredited school and summer enrichment for profoundly gifted students (top 99.9th percentile). Summer courses go far beyond typical high school curricula. Students engage in college-level coursework with expert instructors and a global community of intellectual peers.
programs.note: Requires IQ documentation at the 99.9th percentile or above. Rolling applications year-round. For profoundly gifted students only.

programs.name: Rice University STEM Programs
programs.desc: Multiple programs including STEM@Rice, research apprenticeships, and the BYTE! coding academy. Topics span biomedical hardware, robotics, AI, and engineering. Some programs are competitive and free with stipends; others have moderate tuition.
programs.note: Multiple programs with different deadlines. Check the Rice OCED website for the full listing.

programs.name: Cold Email Research Apprenticeships
programs.dl: Rolling — contact labs now (April is ideal)
programs.desc: Email professors directly at nearby universities to request summer research apprenticeships. This is how many students get their best research experiences. Write a concise, genuine cold email with your resume, transcript, and specific interest in their lab's work. Personalization dramatically improves acceptance rates.
programs.note: April is prime month to reach out — many PIs are finalizing summer lab rosters now. Email 15–20 labs to maximize chances.

events.name: NASA Space Apps Challenge
events.desc: The world's largest hackathon with 55,000+ participants from 160+ countries. Use NASA's open data to solve Earth and space science challenges. Teams of 2–6. Local events in 100+ cities — find one near you at spaceappschallenge.org.

events.name: Hack Club Hackathons (100+)
events.desc: Hack Club maintains the definitive directory of 100+ high school hackathons across 30+ states and 26 countries. Most events are free and cover food. Find events at hackathons.hackclub.com — searchable by location and date.

events.name: HackMIT Blueprint
events.desc: MIT's flagship high school hackathon. Beginner-friendly tracks, workshops taught by MIT undergrads, and hands-on mentorship. Build a project in one weekend alongside 400+ students from across the country.

events.name: Major League Hacking (MLH)
events.desc: The official student hackathon league with 200+ sanctioned events per year, open to high schoolers. Company sponsors include Microsoft, GitHub, and Google. Each MLH event is a chance to meet recruiters and build your developer profile.

events.name: PennApps
events.desc: One of the oldest and most prestigious college hackathons in the US — open to high schoolers. Tracks in software, hardware, AI, and social good. 1,200+ attendees, mentors from top tech companies, and real recruiting connections.

events.name: Regeneron Science Talent Search
events.desc: The nation's most prestigious precollege science competition since 1942. Submit original research in any STEM field. 300 scholars selected nationally; 40 finalists travel to Washington, DC. One of the strongest differentiators for Ivy admissions.

events.name: Regeneron ISEF
events.desc: The world's largest international precollege STEM fair. 1,800+ students from 70+ countries present original research. Must qualify through a regional or state-affiliated fair first. Even regional ISEF qualification is a strong credential for college admissions.

events.name: USACO (USA Computing Olympiad)
events.desc: The top competitive programming competition for high schoolers. Monthly contests: Bronze → Silver → Gold → Platinum → USA IOI team. USACO Gold or Platinum is among the strongest CS credentials for any college application.

events.name: AMC 10/12 → AIME → USAMO
events.desc: The official pathway to the US Math Olympiad and the International Math Olympiad (IMO). AMC 10/12 → AIME → USAMO. AIME qualification is a solid credential; USAMO qualification is exceptional. Prepare using Art of Problem Solving.

events.name: WAICY — World AI Competition for Youth
events.desc: The world's largest AI competition for ages 13–18 with 3,000+ participants from 40+ countries. Tracks include AI project showcase, LLM applications, and AI creative work. Team-based format with mentor feedback rounds. Strong portfolio piece for CS and AI-focused students.

events.name: Kaggle Competitions
events.desc: The world's largest data science community. Open to high schoolers. Start with beginner challenges to build a track record. Even placing in the top 10% of a Kaggle competition is a meaningful CS portfolio piece.

events.name: Breakthrough Junior Challenge
events.desc: Create a 3-minute original video explaining a complex scientific concept in a clear, creative way. Open globally to ages 13–18. One of the largest science prizes for young people. Past winners have explained quantum entanglement, CRISPR, and general relativity.

events.name: Davidson Fellows Scholarship
events.desc: Recognizes students 18 and under who have completed a significant piece of original work in STEM, literature, music, or philosophy. Requires genuinely exceptional, publishable-quality research or creative work. Very few awarded each year.

events.name: JSHS — Junior Science & Humanities Symposium
events.desc: Students present original STEM research to panels of military scientists. Regional → national structure. Top finalists receive substantial scholarships and present at the national symposium. Covers all STEM disciplines. Underrated — competition is less intense than ISEF but highly prestigious.

events.name: Conrad Innovation Summit
events.desc: Teams solve real-world problems using STEM innovation and entrepreneurship, then pitch to NASA astronauts, engineers, and industry leaders at the annual summit. Categories include aerospace, energy, cybersecurity, and health sciences. Open globally to students ages 13–18.
